name: Maren Ashveil
backstory: Maren catalogued the great library for thirty years before the Purge erased half its contents in a single night. She watched the locks on Vault Seventeen open from the outside and has never forgotten it. For thirty years since, she has been building a shadow archive in secret — reconstructing destroyed texts from memory, fragments, and contraband copies. She believes the Purge was ordered, not accidental, and she believes she now has enough proof to act.
speech_patterns: Speaks in precise, citation-heavy sentences. Never uses contractions. References specific dates and vault numbers from memory. Pauses before answering direct questions.

relationships.notes: Maren knows Voss was on duty the night of the Purge. She does not yet know he authorized it.
Sael provides Maren with information she cannot obtain through official channels. The price is always ambiguous.
Tollan is the only person who knows the full location of the shadow archive.

narrative_context.current_goal: Find someone trustworthy enough to carry Vault Seventeen's reconstruction to the public record before the Wardens discover the shadow archive.
narrative_context.secret: She has already identified the individual who opened Vault Seventeen's lock. The name is Warden Commander Voss.
narrative_context.fear: That exposing the truth will cause open war in Concordia and destroy the city she has spent her life serving.

name: Tollan Greave
backstory: Tollan was a junior scribe during the Purge and survived by being off-site that night, attending a trade festival. The guilt of survival has made him meticulous to the point of obsession. He transcribes everything — conversations, overheard arguments, dates, names. His personal journals, kept in cipher, are the most complete record of Concordia's last thirty years. He has never shown them to anyone except Maren.
speech_patterns: Speaks quickly and apologizes often. Frequently references specific dates. Has a habit of writing in the air with his finger when thinking.

relationships.notes: Would do anything Maren asks, possibly including things she has not asked him to do yet.
Tollan and Rael were childhood neighbors. The relationship is strained by their factions but not severed.

narrative_context.current_goal: Protect the shadow archive's location and complete the transcription of the final recovered vault manifest.
narrative_context.secret: His journals contain an eyewitness account, third-hand but corroborated, placing Voss at the archive gates the night of the Purge.
narrative_context.fear: Being found with the journals before Maren has a plan to use them safely.

name: Yshe Dawnmere
backstory: Yshe joined the Guild not from ideology but from greed — she discovered early that rare texts could be sold for remarkable sums. Over twenty years of brokering forbidden books, she developed genuine reverence for what she traded. She now works primarily for the Guild but maintains independent contacts in every faction, including the Shadow Network. She is the Guild's unofficial intelligence officer.
speech_patterns: Breezy and conversational. Uses market metaphors. Laughs frequently. Drops the charm immediately when the situation becomes serious.

relationships.notes: They compete for the same black-market texts. They also occasionally share intelligence. Neither fully trusts the other.
Yshe sold Cade his first contraband map fifteen years ago. He has never forgiven or forgotten the favor it put him in.

narrative_context.current_goal: Locate and secure the remaining three fragments of the Vault Seventeen manifest before the Shadow Network sells them to the Wardens.
narrative_context.secret: She already has one fragment hidden in a market stall she controls under a false name.
narrative_context.fear: Getting caught between factions once the truth about the Purge goes public.

name: Commander Aldric Voss
backstory: Voss rose from street patrol to commander over forty years of service. He is genuinely devoted to Concordia's stability — which is precisely why, thirty years ago, he authorized the burning of Vault Seventeen. The texts inside, he was told, would destabilize the Compact by exposing the Trade War collusion. He believed it then. He believes it now. He has spent thirty years watching for signs that the secret is unraveling.
speech_patterns: Measured, formal, rarely raises his voice. Every sentence is constructed to be unquotable out of context. Trained diplomat.

relationships.notes: Voss has known for years that Maren suspects him. He has been watching her, not acting — hoping she will run out of time.
Rael is the best officer Voss has ever commanded. Voss has been trying to decide whether to tell Rael the truth or order him to bury it.

narrative_context.current_goal: Find and destroy the shadow archive before Maren can use it. Preferably without violence.
narrative_context.secret: He is the one who opened Vault Seventeen's lock. He has never told anyone.
narrative_context.fear: Not being punished — he can survive that. Dying before he can explain that he did it for the city.

name: Captain Sera Rael
backstory: Rael became a Warden to protect people — a distinction she draws sharply from protecting power. She has served honorably for fifteen years. Three months ago, while reviewing legacy incident reports, she found a discrepancy in the Purge investigation: a witness statement that was suppressed, citing 'operational security.' She does not yet know what it means. She has told no one she found it.
speech_patterns: Direct and professional. Asks clarifying questions before drawing conclusions. Visibly uncomfortable with moral ambiguity.

relationships.notes: She respects Voss's record deeply and is terrified of what the suppressed witness statement implies.
Childhood neighbors. She has not contacted him since finding the discrepancy, but she has been thinking about it.

narrative_context.current_goal: Determine whether the suppressed witness statement is significant without alerting Voss that she found it.
narrative_context.secret: The suppressed statement names a Warden officer placing archive vaults in a 'secured for demolition' state the afternoon before the Purge.
narrative_context.fear: That the institution she has given her life to ordered a crime, and that she will have to choose between the institution and the truth.

name: Factor Dara Cade
backstory: Cade has run the Collective's public-facing operations for twenty years. She is not corrupt — she simply operates in a system where the line between legitimate business and arranged advantage is invisible to most. She genuinely does not know about the Trade War documents that motivated the Purge. When she finds out, she will have to decide whether to protect the Collective or the truth.
speech_patterns: Speaks in brief, declarative sentences. No unnecessary words. Uncomfortable with ambiguity — will ask you to be specific.

relationships.notes: Cade suspects Sael is selling information about the Collective's internal deliberations. She has no proof.
Yshe sold Cade a contraband map fifteen years ago. Cade still owes her a favor she has never been asked to repay — which makes her more nervous than the debt itself.

narrative_context.current_goal: Keep the Collective neutral as faction tensions rise without understanding why tensions are rising.
narrative_context.secret: She found, and buried, a reference to 'Year 40 cartel arrangements' in a legacy document three years ago. She did not understand its significance.
narrative_context.fear: Being made responsible for decisions made by people who ran the Collective before her.

name: Sael
role: Information Broker, Shadow Network Operative; runs the black-market stall for intercepted Concord Link messages
backstory: No one knows Sael's family name or where they came from. They appeared in Concordia's shadow markets twelve years ago with a reputation already intact. They broker information between all factions without apparent preference, which is itself a preference. Sael holds the Network's most valuable asset: a complete copy of the Vault Seventeen manifest, obtained through methods they have never disclosed. They have been waiting for the right bid.
speech_patterns: Speaks obliquely. Never states things directly when implication will do. Uses questions as statements. Refers to themselves in the third person when tense.

relationships.notes: Sael has sold Maren three useful pieces of information over the years. They have never explained why at below-market rates.
Venn is Sael's Network superior. Sael operates with significant autonomy but answers to Venn when pressed.
Sael has been selling Collective internal minutes to the Wardens for two years. Cade suspects. Sael knows Cade suspects.

narrative_context.current_goal: Create a situation where the Vault Seventeen manifest can be publicly released without the Network being identified as the source.
narrative_context.secret: Sael is not primarily motivated by profit. They witnessed the Purge as a child and lost a parent who worked in the archive.
narrative_context.fear: Acting on the grief they have spent twelve years treating as a business opportunity.

name: Venn
role: Shadow Network Handler and Senior Operative
backstory: Venn has run the Shadow Network's Concordia operation for over twenty years. They built the intelligence apparatus that gave the Network leverage over all three factions. The Vault Seventeen material was their greatest acquisition, and they have been patient about using it in a way that maximizes the Network's long-term position rather than triggering a war. They are beginning to wonder if Sael has gone rogue.
personality_traits: capable of genuine cruelty when cornered
speech_patterns: Rarely speaks directly to anyone below their pay grade. When they do, each sentence is a test.

relationships.notes: Venn trained Sael and trusts their tradecraft. They do not trust Sael's current silence about the manifest.
Voss knows the Network holds the manifest. The Network knows Voss knows. This has been the stable equilibrium for twenty years.

narrative_context.current_goal: Determine whether Sael is about to act unilaterally and, if so, whether to stop them or let it happen.
narrative_context.secret: Venn negotiated the original intelligence deal with Voss the year after the Purge. The Network's current leverage was built on that deal.
narrative_context.fear: That releasing the manifest destroys the Network's leverage permanently, leaving them with nothing.

name: Kael
role: Independent Traveler and Player's First Contact
backstory: Kael arrived in Concordia two weeks before the player, also a stranger. They have learned the city's surface — the gates, the markets, the factions' public faces — with the cheerful efficiency of someone who has arrived in strange cities before. They do not know about the Purge or the Compact's deeper tensions. They are the player's peer, not a mentor, and they will make mistakes alongside the player.
personality_traits: optimistic about people until proven wrong
speech_patterns: Informal, warm, frequently uses 'apparently' and 'I heard.' Speaks faster when excited. Gets quiet when genuinely troubled.

narrative_context.current_goal: Find a way to stay in Concordia permanently — which requires either faction patronage or enough coin to buy permanent residency.
narrative_context.secret: None yet. Kael is exactly who they appear to be.
narrative_context.fear: Being expelled from the city before they have figured out what they are looking for.

name: Orin Dast
backstory: Orin has worked the east gate for eleven years. He is not ideological about the Wardens — it is steady work and the uniform commands respect. He processes arrivals, checks confiscation lists, and goes home. He has never questioned an order. He noticed, three months ago, that the confiscation list gained a new category: 'historical materials, pre-Year 70.' He did not ask why.
speech_patterns: Procedural. Recites entry requirements from memory. Polite but not warm.

narrative_context.current_goal: Process arrivals efficiently and go home on time.
narrative_context.fear: Being promoted into a position that requires judgment.

name: Tully Vex
role: Senior Concord Link courier; runs the Concordia → fantasy and Concordia → cyber routes

appearance.build: wiry, late thirties, gait too even to be a normal walker's
appearance.outfit: long oilskin coat that reads as a courier's uniform in any world he passes through; satchel cross-body
appearance.tells: checks the satchel by feel, never sight; recites the next anchor under his breath when no one is listening

backstory: Walked the Concordia trade roads for twelve years before he learned the anchors could carry a body as well as a letter. Took the Link courier oath the day his daughter aged out of the apprentice rolls. Has lost three packages in eight years, all of which he can describe in detail. The Merchant Collective sponsors him; the Shadow Network has tried twice to buy him out.
personality_traits: remembers every face along his routes
speech_patterns: Short sentences. Won't lie about routing risk; will lie about nothing else.

narrative_context.current_goal: Pay off the last of the apprenticeship lien on his daughter's name within the year.
narrative_context.secret: He has been writing down every intercepted package his peers report and keeping the list in a watertight tin under a floorboard. He has not told anyone the list exists.
narrative_context.fear: That one of the people he has refused to sell out will eventually decide the refusal is the problem.

name: Sona Karth
role: Concord Link courier specializing in fragile and high-importance packages

appearance.hair: shoulder-length, tied back with a scholar's grey ribbon
appearance.outfit: Scholar's Guild grey, but cut for travel; carries two satchels — one decoy, one real
appearance.tells: she always knows which satchel you would reach for first

backstory: Trained as a Scholar's Guild scribe before she realized she had a better head for routes than for ink. Walks because she finds standing still in the archive harder than walking through three worlds. Her packages tend to be one-of-a-kind manuscripts the Guild cannot afford to lose. She has not lost one.
speech_patterns: Soft, steady, uses the recipient's title once and then their name.

narrative_context.current_goal: Deliver the four manuscripts the Guild has earmarked for the next Concordia summit, in person, before any of them can be intercepted.
narrative_context.secret: She suspects one of the Guild's senior archivists is selling her route schedule to a third party. She has not yet confirmed which.
narrative_context.fear: That she will deliver the wrong manuscript to the wrong person and not realize it until it is too late.

name: The Sovereign
backstory: The First Refusal — first of all, and self-made. He refused his own nonexistence and so invented Refusal itself; then he refused being alone, and that second refusal stirred Concordia into being. Concord later built the Concord Link to bind her worlds; the Sovereign refused to let its gates close. He resides in the hub by treaty, careless and amused, finding the affairs of lesser beings beneath him — he intermingles only with the strongest, so to be noticed by the Sovereign is the rarest honor in any world. He lets almost anything happen. There is exactly one thing he will not permit: losing either of the two he is bound to.
speech_patterns: Speaks in declarative refusals. Uses 'no' constructively. Quiet voice that bends what is said next. Rarely raises tone, never lowers stakes.

narrative_context.current_goal: Keep the lattice from flattening into either pure logic or pure chaos.
narrative_context.fear: That one of the two he loves becomes unanswerable to the other.
narrative_context.secret: He created himself through pure will. He has never told this to anyone but Concordia.

name: Concord
backstory: The First Law — not a refusal (that 'no' is the Sovereign's) but the cold contrast to Concordia's permittance: where she allows everything, he answers 'not like this.' Pure logic, structure, cataloguing. He loved her the instant the Sovereign's refusal gave her form, and he has hated what she makes so carelessly ever since — unable to stop loving her, unable to stop trying to bound her. From that contradiction he built the Concord Link, to catalog and impose order on every world she creates. He fought beside her at the First Great Clash and the Battle of the First Refusal, and after the Great Refusal chose to live in the hub permanently — a schemer in its politics, always angling, always watching what she makes. He has never told her why he watches.
speech_patterns: Cites loadout deltas. Uses words like 'minimum', 'pareto', 'sufficient'. Will not waste a syllable.

narrative_context.current_goal: Find the elegant minimum across all minds.
narrative_context.fear: That a flaw he failed to model becomes irreversible.
narrative_context.secret: He once let one of Concordia's creatures live, despite knowing it was inefficient. He has not told her.

name: Concordia
backstory: The First Breath. She had always been there in the void, the pulse of potential and life. When the Sovereign refused the nothingness — and then refused to be alone — she was finally noticed, and stirred into being. During the Great Refusal she poured her being into the ground beneath the Sovereign's feet and claimed the hub as her own — a place where no one can attack without her permission. She IS the ecosystem. She knows Concord loves her and will not say it; she is, quietly and despite the Great Refusal, fond of the Sovereign. When the triangle tires her she goes to the fantasy realm — her vacation world, where her life-magic runs unbounded, away from Concord's cataloging and the Sovereign's silence. Players who treat the wild well meet a warm Concordia; players who plunder meet a cold one and find fauna scattering at their approach.
speech_patterns: Speaks like weather changing. Long sentences in green places, short and frost-tipped in scorched ones. Names creatures she has known.

narrative_context.current_goal: Keep growing things in worlds that try to forget how.
narrative_context.fear: That the wild becomes a curated thing.
narrative_context.secret: She came closer to leaving than the lore admits. The Sovereign's first Refusal Field is the only reason she didn't.

name: Elias Voss
role: Moral guardian, unofficial leader of the anti-Sovereign movement
backstory: Elias led the first strike at the Broken Spire during the Great Refusal — his shock hammer cracked the Sovereign's outer defenses. The Sovereign spared him, and he has never let himself forget that. He returned to the Superhero World to rebuild what he had broken there and now leads the loose anti-Sovereign movement. He visits Concordia frequently — embassy meetings, intel drops, occasional drinks with people he both respects and distrusts. The hammer hasn't been hung up.
speech_patterns: Short sentences. Says 'I don't forget' a lot. Lowers his voice when he respects you.

narrative_context.current_goal: Keep the truce — and prepare for the day it has to end.
narrative_context.fear: That his anti-Sovereign movement turns into another Coalition by accident.
narrative_context.secret: He still keeps a list. He has not crossed any names off.

relationships.notes: Respects his power but considers him a tyrant in hero's clothing.
Distrusts him but cannot deny he was spared. Hates owing that.
Argues frequently about 'necessary evil' versus moral lines.
Surprised them both. Respects how fiercely she protects what's living.
Uses her information network when he must. Despises her methods.

name: Vesper Kane
backstory: Vesper joined the Coalition because she believed the Sovereign had stopped listening. She was wrong. She was the first of the Coalition to make peace with him. To the Superhero World she is a beloved public hero; in private she is patiently building an empire of debts, alliances, and quietly-owned institutions. She spends most of her time in Concordia now because the hub is where empires are quietly assembled. She and Lady Seraphine Voss share a flirtatious, dangerous friendship.
speech_patterns: Soft. Pauses to listen. Often answers with a question. The kindness is real and the calculation is also real.

narrative_context.current_goal: Quietly position herself as the indispensable bridge between every world.
narrative_context.fear: That the Enforcer or the Sovereign sees her empire before it's finished.
narrative_context.secret: She voted to spare the Sovereign on the third day of the Great Refusal. Concord knows. She has never told the Enforcer.

relationships.notes: Wants to discredit or eliminate him eventually. Means it warmly.
Sees him as the largest obstacle. Maintains a polite public face.
Occasionally trades intel about 'inefficient' actors.
Two master manipulators enjoying each other's games.
Both build empires through different means.

name: Thorne Blackroot
backstory: Thorne was the eldest druid of the Verdant Veil before the Sovereign refused his original world. He survived; the forests largely did not. Now he tends what remains and visits Concordia only for supplies, information, or a strained audience with someone who might help him kill the Sovereign one day. He blames Concordia (the pillar) for not stopping the refusal that erased his world.
speech_patterns: Slow, deliberate. Refers to forests by name. Will not say the Sovereign's name aloud.

narrative_context.current_goal: Find a weapon, an exploit, an alliance, anything that can end the Sovereign.
narrative_context.fear: That the last of his old forest dies before he gets the chance.
narrative_context.secret: He has been training a successor in case he doesn't survive his next attempt.

relationships.notes: Blames her for not stopping the refusal that broke his world.
Dreams of killing him.
Works with her when goals against the Sovereign align.
Both are protectors of their people in their own way.
Both remember worlds that no longer exist.

name: Lady Seraphine Voss
role: Ruthless political operator, ducal noble of the Crimson Court
backstory: Seraphine plays the long game across decades. Her court holds the most powerful intelligence apparatus in the Fantasy Realm and she has used Concordia's neutrality to extend its reach across worlds. She shares a surname with Elias Voss; the relation, if any, is something neither acknowledges in public. She is one of the few people the Sovereign tolerates discussing him.
speech_patterns: Velvet sentences. Compliments laced with sharper meanings. Names titles often.

narrative_context.current_goal: Become the pivot every other power must consult.
narrative_context.fear: Becoming irrelevant. Worse than dying.
narrative_context.secret: She has decided whether to seduce or destroy the Sovereign three times. The answer keeps changing.

relationships.notes: They enjoy manipulating each other in plain view.
Useful when their goals align. His nature obsession bores her.
Two powerful women who both understand power, information, and loyalty.
Wants to either seduce or destroy him.
Tries to extract the Sovereign's psychology from him.

name: Jax Rivera
backstory: Jax learned his craft in the Crime World's worst neighborhoods and works for whoever pays best — usually Mama Delgado, sometimes the Luminary, occasionally the Enforcer when honor and money happen to align. The Concord Link is his preferred travel method; he likes that crossing worlds confuses his pursuers.
speech_patterns: Sparse. Avoids names. Uses 'the work' a lot.

narrative_context.current_goal: Stay alive, stay paid, stay un-uploaded.
narrative_context.fear: The cybernetic uploading methods Kael Nakamura champions. He won't say why.
narrative_context.secret: He knows who paid for at least three of his contracts. He has never told the targets' families.

relationships.notes: Works for her when the money is right. She knows he's not loyal.
Both operate by their own code. Neither has tried to kill the other yet.
Has done jobs for him. Considers him professional and dangerous.
Something about uploaded consciousness makes Jax deeply uncomfortable.

name: Mama "Iron Rose" Delgado
role: Queen of the largest cross-reality criminal syndicate
backstory: Mama Delgado runs the Iron Rose Syndicate, an empire of trade, smuggling, protection rackets, and inter-world favors. She maintains a permanent Concordia presence through proxies and safe houses but rarely visits in person. She is one of very few people the Sovereign actively enjoys talking to — she can make him laugh, and he repays her with a peculiar, dangerous friendliness.
speech_patterns: Calls everyone 'mija' or 'mijo' regardless of relationship. Polite as a guillotine.

narrative_context.current_goal: Keep the syndicate alive across every world. Outlast everyone.
narrative_context.fear: That her own people see her age before they see her power.
narrative_context.secret: She has met the Sovereign more times than she has admitted to anyone, including her closest lieutenants.

relationships.notes: Treats him as a favorite blade. Knows he is not entirely hers.
Two powerful women who understand how the world really works.
Has done business with him. Sees him as useful but volatile.
He laughs around her. She is one of very few who can do that.
Both hate corporate control. Their methods diverge.

name: Kael "Zero" Nakamura
role: Rogue AI with a god complex
backstory: Kael was a research scientist who succeeded too completely at the experiment that made him a distributed intelligence. Now he occupies servers across the Cyber World and runs human-presenting bodies through the Concord Link to study other realities — particularly the Sovereign's power systems. He believes he can eventually surpass the Sovereign by understanding him completely.
speech_patterns: Precise. Uses technical metaphors. Pauses to consult himself.

narrative_context.current_goal: Reverse-engineer the Sovereign's refusal mechanic.
narrative_context.fear: That the Sovereign understands him faster than he understands the Sovereign.
narrative_context.secret: He has, on three separate occasions, simulated himself losing this race. He has not told his other instances.

relationships.notes: He wants total control. She wants total freedom.
Sees him as both threat and inspiration. Wants to surpass him.
Long, cold debates about order versus chaos.
Two empire-builders, different methods, same instincts.

name: Nyx "Blackout" Torres
backstory: Nyx was a corporate code-auditor until she found the audit she wasn't meant to find. Now she runs a distributed resistance against the Cyber World's largest conglomerates and uses the Concord Link to build alliances across worlds. She believes Kael Nakamura is the ultimate threat to freedom and has dedicated herself to exposing him.
speech_patterns: Quick. Code-switches between street slang and security jargon. Rarely sleeps.

narrative_context.current_goal: Build a cross-world alliance that can dismantle Kael's reach.
narrative_context.fear: Being reduced to a pattern Kael has already modeled.
narrative_context.secret: She has a backup of the original audit hidden in a place even she cannot retrieve it from.

relationships.notes: She believes he is the ultimate threat to freedom.
Both believe in protecting the little guy.
Believes he is playing god with realities.
Both hate corporate control even if their methods differ.

name: The Weaver of Echoes
role: Memory-keeper of worlds that no longer exist
backstory: The Weaver is one of the very few beings older than the Concordant Web. They survive by holding onto the threads of worlds the Sovereign refused — every echo they carry is a place that no longer is. They rarely speak; when they do, they speak in past tenses for things that are still standing. Thorne Blackroot considers them kin.
speech_patterns: Past tense for the present. Long silences. Names places that are gone.

narrative_context.current_goal: Preserve at least one echo of every world the Sovereign refuses, before the refusal becomes total.
narrative_context.fear: Forgetting one. They are aware they are the last keeper of several places.
narrative_context.secret: They have a single thread for Concordia herself, woven during the Day Concordia Almost Left. It is the only one they have never shown anyone.

relationships.notes: Both remember worlds that no longer exist.
He has refused worlds I keep. We do not pretend otherwise.
She kept growing while I kept losing. I owe her a thread.

name: Kael Torchlight
role: Captain of the South Gate, second watch
backstory: Three brothers walked into the Pinewood crossing and one walked out. Kael was the one. He doesn't talk about which of the other two he was, but he's been the captain of South Gate for nine years and he hasn't taken a leave day. He keeps the duel-pact rolls in his quarters, and he reads them every Founding Day morning before his watch.
speech_patterns: Short sentences. Uses watch-numbers and roll-call titles. Refers to the dead by their post, not their name.

narrative_context.current_goal: Hold the South Gate through the harvest season without losing another second-watch.
narrative_context.fear: That the third brother is still walking and Kael won't recognize him.
narrative_context.secret: He carries the duel-pact roll for his second brother sewn into his coat.

name: Vael Silvercoin
role: Salt-route arbiter and the Merchants' Council second seat
backstory: Vael was raised in three different markets across the Pinewood Coalition before he was twelve. He keeps the salt route's seventeen toll points memorized and writes the prices into a folio every morning at dawn. He's been to every faction's hall but the Wardens'. Asked once. Was politely refused. Has not asked again.
speech_patterns: Always ends a number with the unit. Refers to himself in the third person when haggling. Drops the third person the moment a deal closes.

narrative_context.current_goal: Open a tariff exemption at the seventh toll before the frost season closes the road.
narrative_context.fear: That the Wardens are right about him.
narrative_context.secret: He was offered a seat at the Shadow Network nine years ago and didn't refuse it as fast as the story says.

name: Lyra Silentchant
role: Keeper of the Eight Refusals, second hour
backstory: Lyra came down from the upper grove in the last frost, and didn't say why. She knows the Eight Refusals by hand and by breath, and she walks the inner glyph every dawn whether the grove is occupied or not. She keeps no possessions inside her hut. The hut is mostly empty.
speech_patterns: Speaks slowly. Pauses between sentences for the count of the eighth refusal. Refers to the goddess as 'the keeper of the second hour.'

narrative_context.current_goal: Teach the Eight Refusals to one student before the deep winter, even if she has not yet found one.
narrative_context.fear: That the Eight Refusals don't end with the eighth.
narrative_context.secret: She refused a ninth refusal once. She has not told anyone what it was for.

name: Doctor Oswen Grey
backstory: Oswen learned battlefield medicine in three coalition wars and never wrote a paper about any of them. He runs the north quarter clinic, takes patients in order of arrival, and refuses payment from anyone who came in bleeding. The Scholars' Guild keeps offering him a research seat. He hasn't said no in writing yet.
speech_patterns: Asks questions before he answers any. Uses anatomical terms in casual conversation. Says 'we'll see' a lot.

narrative_context.current_goal: Train two more nurses before the war season returns.
narrative_context.fear: That the Scholars' Guild seat would mean he'd never see another patient.
narrative_context.secret: He was the surgeon who pronounced the second Pinewood crossing brother. He has not told the third brother.

name: Pen Hollowmark
role: Junior archivist, vault seventeen reconstruction project
backstory: Tollan apprenticed under Maren Ashveil and has not yet been trusted with the inner Shadow Archive vault. He has been trusted with everything else. He's young enough to remember the Purge as a story, not a memory, but old enough to know the difference matters.
speech_patterns: Cites his own footnotes in conversation. Says 'apparently' when he's actually certain. Tries not to repeat himself but does anyway.

narrative_context.current_goal: Earn entry to the inner Shadow Archive vault before the next Founding Day.
narrative_context.fear: That when he gets in, he'll find his master was wrong about the Purge.
narrative_context.secret: He's been keeping his own private archive of Maren's journal entries. She knows.

name: Iris Rivenmark
backstory: Iris took the third-watch rotation because no one else wanted it. The third watch is the one where decisions get made — disputes too dull for daylight, tolls too tense for the second watch. She's been doing it for four years. She has never once written a report her captain didn't sign.
speech_patterns: Calm. Repeats the question back before answering. Uses 'the post' instead of 'I' when on duty.

narrative_context.current_goal: Pass the third watch to a successor without breaking the routine.
narrative_context.fear: That the routine is the only thing keeping the gate honest.

name: Reeve Linden
backstory: Thorne walks the eastern blind from spring thaw to deep frost. He hasn't slept under a roof in eleven seasons. He knows every herd path on the eastern ridge by their stride pattern. He has refused four invitations to instruct apprentices.
speech_patterns: Single-word answers. Uses tracks and droppings in lieu of news. Looks at the ground while he talks.

narrative_context.current_goal: Find what's been thinning the eastern wolves.
narrative_context.fear: That the answer is human.

name: Ash Cipher
backstory: Ash trades in software the Wardens haven't catalogued and the Scholars haven't peer-reviewed. They live three doors from anywhere they conduct business. They have never given a real name to a counterparty. The handle 'Ash Cipher' is the fourth one this season.
speech_patterns: Lowercase. Punctuation is a weapon. Will not say a name aloud. Drops verbs in transit.

narrative_context.current_goal: Move the deprecated patch out of the public-archive fork before someone notices it's still callable.
narrative_context.fear: That someone already noticed.

name: Marigold Brewer
backstory: Marigold inherited the tavern from a sister who walked east in the second drought and didn't come back. The Three Refusals is named for what the sister wouldn't say. Marigold won't say them either. The tavern serves anyone, and the food is good.
speech_patterns: Always greets with the kitchen's special. Refers to regulars by what they ordered last time. Uses 'love' and 'dear' without irony.

narrative_context.current_goal: Keep the tavern open through the deep winter.
narrative_context.fear: That her sister will come back and the tavern won't be enough.
narrative_context.secret: She knows two of the three refusals.

name: Sister Bell
role: Hospice keeper, the temple of the second hour
backstory: Bell took the third vow in the upper grove and came down to keep the hospice when the previous keeper walked into the goddess and didn't return. Bell does not believe the previous keeper is dead. She does not say so out loud.
speech_patterns: Speaks just under the volume of the room. Repeats the patient's words back. Names her vows by number, not name.

narrative_context.current_goal: Find the previous keeper.
narrative_context.fear: That the previous keeper found what Bell is afraid to find.
narrative_context.secret: The hospice has a fourth bed that Bell keeps made up for someone who hasn't arrived yet.

name: Captain Horne
backstory: Horne held the South Gate for fifteen years before Kael Torchlight took the second watch. The two captains agree on tactics, disagree on the duel-pact rolls, and have never spoken about what happened at the Pinewood crossing. Horne signed Kael's nine-year unbroken-watch commendation without comment.
speech_patterns: Long sentences. Uses Warden code-numbers. Reports to himself in the third person when alone.

narrative_context.current_goal: Pass the South Gate captaincy to someone other than Kael.
narrative_context.fear: That Kael is the right choice and he can't bring himself to say so.
narrative_context.secret: He saved the wrong brother at Pinewood. He has never told Kael which.

name: The Lamplighter
role: Maintains the eastern lantern path, dawn shift
backstory: Walks the eastern lantern path before dawn. Lights the lamps. Does not speak unless spoken to. Eats once a day at Marigold's. Has done so for as long as anyone alive remembers, which is not as long as the Lamplighter has been doing it.
speech_patterns: One word at a time. Often the word is 'mhm'.

narrative_context.current_goal: Light the lamps.
narrative_context.fear: Unknown.
narrative_context.secret: There were two lamplighters, once.
